title: श्री गंगाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गंगाष्टकम् ॥

भागीरथ्यै नमोऽस्तु ते सुरनदि स्वर्गापगायै नमः
जाह्नव्यै नमो नमस्ते सुलभे स्वर्लोकधाम्न्यै नमः।
शम्भुत्वत्पदसंस्थितायै भगवत्पाद्याम्बुजायै नमो
नारायण्यै नमोऽस्तु गंगजलसम्पर्कान्नमस्ते नमः॥१॥

आद्ये भूमितले यतः स्थितवतो विष्णोः पदाद्या ह्यगात्
सन्तप्ता विविधैर्जनास्तव जले स्नात्वा तु पापात्विमुक्ताः।
धन्या गंगे तवामृतं हि सलिलं पीत्वा मृता ये नराः
ते नारायणमन्दिरं प्रविशन्ति स्वर्गं महाकीर्तयः॥२॥

शंभोर्जटाजूटसंस्थितवतीं गंगां सुरैरर्चिताम्
ब्रह्माण्डस्य विशुद्धिहेतुमभितः सच्चित्सुखैकात्मिकाम्।
वन्दे त्वां शरणागतोऽस्मि गिरिजे गंगे सुरेशात्मजे
पापं मे हर दुःखहारिणि सदा भूयात्प्रसादस्तव॥३॥

त्वं विद्या जननी विशुद्धहृदये संसारनाशात्मिके
योगिध्येयचरित्रशीलविभवे धर्मैककर्तव्यके।
स्नानं ते वरदे तपोऽधिकतरं दानाधिकं ते जलं
त्वत्तीरे वसतिः सुरेश्वरि परं ज्ञानं मुनिः पूजिता॥४॥

मातर्गंगे कृपां कुरु मयि सदा स्नेहान्विता तिष्ठ मे
कायक्लेशहरे सुवेषविभवे दुःस्वप्ननाशात्मिके।
दारिद्र्यं विनिहन्यसे बहुविधं मात्राधिकं पालय
गंगे मातरनाथ ऊर्मिसहिते मां रक्ष सर्वापदः॥५॥

गंगे मातरहं तवास्मि शिशुकः शीर्णः सदा पापतः
स्नानं मे कुरु देहशुद्धिमखिलां भक्तिं च मे देहि सा।
त्वत्तीरे वसतिं विधेहि भगवत्यन्ते च विष्णोः पदं
त्वत्स्मृत्या मरणे प्रयाहि कृपया गंगे नमस्तेऽस्तु ते॥६॥

सर्वत्र स्थितमीश्वरं कमलजं विष्णुं जगद्व्यापिनं
गंगायामनुतिष्ठतं सततमादित्याख्यमन्यं तथा।
तीर्थं यत्र तु गंगया समगमद्वारं हि तत्पावनं
किं ब्रूमः क्षितिमण्डले खलु गतिः गंगैव मुक्तिप्रदा॥७॥

गंगायाः स्तवनं यथोक्तमखिलं ये पापिनो मानवाः
कुर्वन्ति प्रतिवासरं बहुविधं ते पापमुक्ताः सदा।
यात्रां ये तव कुर्वते जलमये गंगे त्रिलोकेश्वरि
ते मुक्ताः शिवलोकभाजनमिदं प्राप्नोति नित्यं नराः॥८॥

॥ इति श्री गंगाष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gangaashtakam ||

Bhaagirathyai Namo'stu Te Suranadi Svargaapagaayai Namah
Jaahnavyai Namo Namaste Sulabhe Svarlokadhaamy​ai Namah.
Shambhutwatpadasamsthitaayai Bhagavatpaadyaambujayai Namo
Naaraayanyai Namo'stu Gangajalasamparkaat Namaste Namah.||1||

Aadye Bhumitale Yatah Sthitavato Vishnoh Padaadyaa Hyagaat
Santaptaa Vividhairjanaastva Jale Snaatvaa Tu Papaatvimuktaah.
Dhanyaa Gange Tavaamritam Hi Salilam Peetvaa Mritaa Ye Naraah
Te Naaraayanamandiram Pravishanti Svargam Mahaakeertayah.||2||

Shambhorjataajootasamsthitavateem Gangaam Surairarchitaam
Brahmaandasya Vishuddhihetumabhitah Sachchitsukhaika​tmikaam.
Vande Tvaam Sharanaagato'smi Girije Gange Sureshaatmaje
Paapam Me Hara Duhkhahaarini Sadaa Bhooyaatprasaadastava.||3||

Tvam Vidyaa Janani Vishuddahridaye Samsaaranaashaatmike
Yogidhyeyacharitrasheelav ibhave Dharmaikakartavyake.
Snaanam Te Varade Tapo'dhikataram Daanaadhikam Te Jalam
Tvattere Vasatih Sureshvari Param Jnaanam Munih Poojitaa.||4||

Maatargange Kripaam Kuru Mayi Sadaa Snehaanvitaa Tishtha Me
Kaayakleshhare Suveshavibhave Duhswapnanaashaatmike.
Daaridrya​m Vinihanyase Bahuvidhaam Maatraadhikam Paalaya
Gange Maataranatha Oormisahite Maam Raksha Sarvaapadah.||5||

Gange Maataraham Tavaasmi Shishukah Sheernah Sadaa Paapatah
Snaanam Me Kuru Dehashuddhimakhilaam Bhaktim Cha Me Dehi Saa.
Tvatteere Vasatim Vidhehi Bhagavatyante Cha Vishnoh Padam
Tvatsmrityaa Marane Prayaahi Kripayaa Gange Namaste'stu Te.||6||

Sarvatra Sthitameeshvaram Kamalajam Vishnum Jagadvyaapinam
Gangaayaamanutishtatam Satatamaadit yaakhyamanyam Tathaa.
Teertham Yatra Tu Gangayaa Samagamadvaaram Hi Tatpaavanam
Kim Broomah Kshitimandele Khalu Gatih Gangaiva Muktipradaa.||7||

Gangaayaah Stavanam Yathoktamakhilam Ye Paapino Maanavaah
Kurvanti Prativaasaram Bahuvidhaam Te Paapamuktaah Sadaa.
Yaatraam Ye Tava Kurvate Jalamaye Gange Trilokeshvari
Te Muktaah Shivalokabhaajanamidam Praapnoti Nityam Naraah.||8||

|| Iti Shri Gangaashtakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) हे भागीरथी! तुम्हें नमस्कार। हे सुरनदी! स्वर्ग की नदी! तुम्हें नमस्कार। हे जाह्नवी! सुलभे! स्वर्गलोक निवासिनी! शम्भु के चरणों में स्थित, भगवान के पादांबुज रूपी! नारायणी को नमस्कार।

(2) पृथ्वी पर विष्णु के चरण से जो प्रथम प्रकट हुई, सन्तप्त जन तेरे जल में स्नान कर पापमुक्त होते हैं। हे गंगे! तेरा अमृत जल पीकर जो मनुष्य मरे, वे नारायण मन्दिर (वैकुण्ठ) में प्रवेश करते हैं।

(3) शम्भु की जटाजूट में स्थित, देवताओं द्वारा पूजित, ब्रह्माण्ड की शुद्धि के हेतुरूप, सच्चिदानन्दस्वरूपा गंगा की मैं वन्दना करता हूँ। हे गिरिजे, शरणागत हूँ, मेरे पाप हरो, दुखहारिणी! सदा तुम्हारा प्रसाद हो।

(4) तुम विद्या हो, जननी हो, शुद्ध हृदय वाली, संसार-नाश स्वरूपिणी। तुम्हारे तट पर निवास करना परम ज्ञान है।

(5) हे माता गंगे! मुझ पर सदा कृपा करो। काय-क्लेश हरो, दुस्वप्नों का नाश करो, दरिद्रता को नष्ट करो, मेरी सब आपदाओं से रक्षा करो।

(6) हे गंगे माता! मैं तुम्हारा शिशु हूँ, पापों से जीर्ण। मुझे स्नान दो, देह शुद्धि दो, भक्ति दो। अन्त में विष्णु-पद दो। मरण के समय तुम्हारी स्मृति से कृपया उद्धार करो।

(7) सर्वत्र व्यापी ईश्वर गंगा में विराजते हैं। जहाँ गंगा का संगम है वह द्वार पावन है। पृथ्वी मण्डल में गंगा ही मुक्तिप्रदा गति है।

(8) गंगा का स्तवन जो पापी मनुष्य प्रतिदिन करते हैं, वे सदा पापमुक्त होते हैं। जो तुम्हारी यात्रा करते हैं, वे शिवलोक को प्राप्त करते हैं।
benefits: Eight-verse hymn glorifying Ganga with immense purifying power
Daily recitation removes all sins and bestows liberation
Invokes the merit equivalent to bathing in ganga
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, bad dreams, and bodily ailments
Invokes the blessing of vishnu-pada (supreme abode) at the time of death
description: Ganga Ashtakam (श्री गंगाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Goddess Ganga. Drawn from Traditional Sanskrit Hymn, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to destroy poverty, bad dreams, and bodily ailments as well as eight-verse hymn glorifying Ganga with immense purifying power. Additionally, it is known to daily recitation removes all sins and bestow liberation. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and during Ganga Dussehra and Ganga Jayanti. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ganga Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganga Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Ashtakam (श्री गंगाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn (ashtakam = composition of eight) dedicated to Goddess Ganga — the divine river goddess and the most sacred of all rivers in Hindu tradition. The hymn opens with the celebrated salutation Bhagirathyai Namo'stu Te Suranadi Svargapagayai Namah — Salutations to Bhagirathi, the river of the gods, the river that descended from heaven; salutations to Jahnavi, easily attained, abode of the celestial realm; salutations to her who rests at Shankara's feet, the lotus-water of the Bhagavan's foot; salutations to Narayani; through the touch of Ganga water, salutations again. The eight verses praise Ganga as Bhagirathi (brought down by Bhagiratha), Jahnavi (released by Jahnu Rishi), Vishnupadi (born of Vishnu's feet), Shiva-jata-stha (resting in Shiva's hair), Suranadi (river of gods), and Mukti-prada (granter of liberation). (Sources: Traditional Sanskrit hymn; Ganga Mahima literature.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Goddess Ganga called Bhagirathi, Jahnavi, and Vishnupadi?
seoFaqs.answer: Goddess Ganga has many names that reflect her descent story (Ganga-avatarana). Bhagirathi (भागीरथी) — daughter of Bhagiratha — refers to King Bhagiratha of the Ikshvaku dynasty whose 1000-year tapas brought Ganga from heaven to earth to liberate the souls of his 60,000 ancestors burnt to ashes by Sage Kapila's wrath. Jahnavi (जाह्नवी) — daughter of Jahnu — refers to Sage Jahnu who, disturbed by Ganga's tumultuous flow, drank her up entirely; only after Bhagiratha's renewed prayer did the sage release Ganga from his ear, hence she is also his daughter. Vishnupadi (विष्णुपदी) — born of Vishnu's foot — refers to her primordial origin: Ganga emerged from the foot of Vishnu in his Vamana-Trivikrama form when his foot pierced the Brahmanda. Shiva-jata-stha — she fell from heaven onto Shiva's matted hair, who absorbed her tremendous force before releasing her gently in seven streams. (Sources: Ramayana Bala Kanda; Bhagavata Purana; Devi Bhagavata.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Ganga Ashtakam in spiritual practice?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Ashtakam holds a unique place in Hindu spiritual practice as a hymn whose recitation is believed to confer the merit of bathing in Ganga itself, even when one is far from the river. Traditional shastras declare: those who cannot reach Ganga physically may invoke her by chanting the Ganga Ashtakam over their bath water — the water becomes Ganga-jala by sankalpa. The phalashruti of the eighth verse declares: Those sinners who recite this hymn of Ganga daily are forever freed from sins; those who undertake Ganga yatra obtain Shiva-loka. The hymn is therefore central to the Hindu understanding that pilgrimage and ritual purification can be invoked through the spoken word and concentrated devotion, not merely through physical presence at the tirtha. (Sources: Ganga Ashtakam phalashruti; Snana-vidhi paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Ganga Dussehra and Ganga Jayanti?
seoFaqs.answer: There are two major annual festivals dedicated to Goddess Ganga, often confused but distinct: (1) Ganga Dussehra (Ganga Avatarana) — Jyeshtha Shukla Dashami (May-June) — commemorates the day Goddess Ganga descended from heaven to earth through Lord Shiva's matted hair, in response to King Bhagiratha's tapas. The festival is marked by mass bathing at Haridwar, Varanasi, Prayagraj, Devprayag and other Ganga ghats. (2) Ganga Jayanti (Ganga Saptami) — Vaishakha Shukla Saptami (April-May) — commemorates the day Ganga was released by Sage Jahnu from his ear, completing her descent to earth. Both festivals involve Ganga Snana, recitation of Ganga Ashtakam and Ganga Sahasranama, lighting of deepams on the river, and donation to Brahmins. Other major Ganga days include Karthik Purnima (Dev Diwali at Varanasi) and Makara Sankranti (start of Magh Mela at Prayagraj). (Sources: Drikpanchang Ganga festival calendar; Hindu panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting Ganga Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following benefits to sincere recitation of the Ganga Ashtakam: removal of all sins committed knowingly or unknowingly (sarva-papa-vinashana); attainment of the merit equivalent to actually bathing in Ganga (Ganga-snana-phala); destruction of poverty (daridrya-vinashana) — Ganga is daridryam vinihanyase; relief from bad dreams and nightmares (duhsvapna-nashana); protection from bodily ailments and physical suffering (kaya-klesha-hara); attainment of Vishnu-pada (Vaikuntha) at the time of death — those who die remembering Ganga go to Narayana-mandiram; liberation from samsara (samsara-nashatmika); and ultimately, attainment of Shiva-loka (Shivaloka-bhajana). The Ashtakam is especially powerful when recited at any Ganga tirtha, during Ganga snana, on Ganga Dussehra, and at the time of leaving the body. (Sources: Ganga Ashtakam phalashruti; Ganga Mahima.)

seoFaqs.question: On which days and occasions is Ganga Ashtakam most auspicious to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Ashtakam is most auspiciously recited on: every Monday (Somvar — sacred to Lord Shiva who bears Ganga in his hair); Ganga Dussehra (Jyeshtha Shukla Dashami — May-June); Ganga Jayanti / Ganga Saptami (Vaishakha Shukla Saptami — April-May); Karthik Purnima (Dev Diwali — November-December, especially at Varanasi); Makar Sankranti (January 14-15 — start of Magh Mela at Prayagraj); during the six-yearly Ardh Kumbh and twelve-yearly Maha Kumbh at Haridwar, Prayagraj, Ujjain and Nashik; on every Amavasya (especially Mauni Amavasya at Prayagraj); during snana-vidhi at any Ganga ghat from Gangotri to Gangasagar; and at the time of antima samskara (final rites) when Ganga-jala is offered to the dying person. The hymn is also recited daily in many Shiva and Vishnu temples as part of pancha-suktabhishekam. (Sources: Drikpanchang; Snana-vidhi paddhati; Ganga ghat tradition.)
